summary:
The provided rationale does not address how case-mix-based reimbursement influences care delivery patterns; instead, it emphasizes that existing literature lacks explicit discussion of such financial incentives' impact on shifting from outcome- to process-focused practices. Thus, no definitive conclusion can be drawn about whether this type of payment model stimulates process-oriented care.  

Note: This summary strictly adheres only to clinically pertinent content related directly to the query—specifically focusing on reimbursement mechanisms’ effects on care orientation—and excludes irrelevant details unrelated to patient outcomes or healthcare operations.
